translations.translation:
Liu Ba (Zichu)
劉巴 (子初)
Lived AD 173–222
aSanguozhiBiographyRecords of the Three Kingdoms [三國志]
Scroll 39 (Shu 9)
byZL181
onMarch 5th, 2025

Liu Ba, styledZichu, was fromZhengyang[烝陽],Lingling[零陵].
He had a renowned reputation when he was young.ATheGovernorofJing province[荊州牧],Liu Biao, repeatedly recruited him and he was also nominated asOutstanding Talent, all of which he did not accept [荊州]. When[Liu] Biaodied,Excellency Caowent on an expedition againstJing province[荊州]. TheFormer Lordfled to theJiangnan region[江南] and many scholars of theJingchu region[荊楚] followed him like clouds [gathering].[Liu] Bawent north toExcellency Cao.Excellency Caorecruited him to serve as abureaucrat. He was dispatched to attract and admitChangsha[長沙],Lingling[零陵], andGuiyang[桂陽].BIt happened thatFormer Lordcaptured those three commanderies, and[Liu] Bawas unable to report back his mission. He then went afar intoJiaozhi[交阯].CTheFormer Lordthus was deeply resentful.[Liu] Balater arrived in Shu [蜀] fromJiaozhi[交阯].DSoon after, theFormer LordsettledYi province[益州].[Liu] Baapologized for his crimes and faults, and theFormer Lorddid not rebuke him.EZhugeKongmingrecommended him several times, so theFormer Lordrecruited him to serve asbureaucrat of the Western Bureauunder theGeneral of the Left.F
TheEminent Worthies of Linglingstates: The grandfather of[Liu] Ba, [Liu] Yao [劉曜], wasGrand AdministratorofCangwu[蒼梧太守]. His father,[Liu] Xiang, wasGrand AdministratorofJiangxia[江夏太守] andGeneral who Sweeps Away Enemies. At the time,Sun Jianraised an army to suppressDong Zhuo. Because theGrand AdministratorofNanyang[南陽太守],Zhang Zi, did not give him with army supplies, he killed him.[Liu] Xiangwas of the selfsame heart, and hence, the people ofNanyang[南陽] resented[Liu] Xiang. They raised troops to attack him, and he fought, but lost and died.Liu Biaoalso never liked[Liu] Xiang, so he detained[Liu] Baand intended to kill him. He sent those who were formerly trusted by[Liu] Xiangto[Liu] Baseveral times to secretly entice him, saying, "GovernorLiuwants to put you in danger! You should follow us into hiding!” It was like this three times, but[Liu] Baalways never answered, and they reported this back to[Liu] Biao.[Liu] Biaothus did not kill[Liu] Ba. When he was eighteen, the commandery appointed him to serve as scribe of the census bureau,Master of Notes, andMaster of Records.Liu Xianwanted to sendZhou Buyito[Liu] Bato study, but[Liu] Bareplied:
“When I travelled in theJingbei region[荊北] in the past, I had been a teacher, yet I would recite my memory [of texts] in answering questions during education,1and thus am unworthy of being notable. Within me, I do not have the skill of remaining in quietude like Yang Zhu [楊朱],2and outside me, I do not have the demeanor of being engaged in current affairs like Mo Di [墨翟].3I am like the South Sieve of the heavens: pretty, yet useless!4You granted me a letter with the intent to have yourworthy nephewruin his magnificence of luanniaos and fenghuangs, and rove about in the world of swallows and sparrows: how would this enlighten him? I am ashamed of [the maxim] ‘having, and yet acting as lacking; and full, and yet acting as empty’!5How could I bear such [atask]?”•••
This refers to a teacher who answered questions with quotations of ancient texts or from what he learned, without offering any new opinions of his own as an answer. This was frowned upon in Ruism.↩
Yang Zhu [楊朱] favored a philosophy where people kept to themselves, refraining from helping or harming any other people.↩
Mo Di [墨翟] favored a philosophy where people were altruistic and cared for all people, in a way being outgoing.↩
The “South Sieve” [南箕] was an asterism in Chinese astronomy, but is a metaphor for something useless. This is referencing a line from theBook of Odes: “Trembling and diverging [stars] can be made into the South Sieve.” [哆兮侈兮。成是南箕。] Doubly useless in that it has no practical use; it cannot do what an actual sieve could do, so though it looks pretty, it is completely useless.↩
This is basically a convoluted expression stating that one should be humble despite being very talented.Liu Bathinks that he lacks that virtue.↩
TheEminent Worthies of Linglingstates: WhenExcellency Caowas defeated atWulin[烏林] and returned north, he wanted to sendHuan Jie, but[Huan] Jiedeclined, that he was inferior to[Liu] Ba.[Liu] BatoldExcellency Cao, “Liu BeioccupyingJing province[荊州] must not be so!” TheExcellencysaid, “If[Liu] Beiplans against us, I will have the six armies go after him.”
TheEminent Worthies of Linglingstates: When[Liu] Bawent toLingling[零陵], he could not complete his deed, so he wanted to travel toJiao province[交州], then take the roads to return back to the capital. At the time,Zhuge Liangwas atLinzheng[臨烝].[Liu] Bawrote to[Zhuge] Liang, “After undergoing peril and experiencing hardship, I have come across people with righteousness on their mind. For them to go to themajoritywith me, undertake the intentions of Heaven, and follow the nature of living things are not deeds my wisdom is capable of encouraging. If I am at the end of the route and have done everything I could about such, then I shall tender my life to the azure seas and never look back atJing province[荊州].6”[Zhuge] Liangfollowed him and told him, "Lord Liuis a mighty talent and among the best of this world. He has occupied the lands ofJing[荊], and there is no one who has not submitted to his virtue. Heaven and Mankind already know who to leave and who to join. Where do you intend to go?”[Liu] Basaid, “I received orders, so I came, and if I cannot complete them, then I should return. That is the proper way. Why do you have to say suchwords?”•••
This seems to be an expression of sailing on the rivers to return toCao Cao.↩
TheEminent Worthies of Linglingstates: When[Liu] BaenteredJiaozhi[交阯], he changed his surname to Zhang. He and theGrand AdministratorofJiaozhi[交阯太守],Shi Xie, discussed plans, but were not in agreement, so he left from the roads ofZangke[牂牁]. He was detained byYizhou commandery[益州郡], and theGrand Administratorwanted to kill him. TheMaster of Recordssaid, “This is no ordinary man. Do not kill him!” TheMaster of Recordsinvited to escort him to the province [seat], and they met theGovernorofYi province[益州牧],[Liu] Zhang. The father of[Liu] Zhang,[Liu] Yan, had been nominated asFilial and Incorruptby the father of[Liu] Ba,[Liu] Xiang, so when he met[Liu] Ba, he was shocked and happy, and each time there were major affairs, he would always consult him.
Your minister,[Pei] Songzhi, would inspect this.Liu Yan, during the reign of theKeenEmperorofHan, had already served asMinister of the Imperial ClanandMinister of Ceremonies, and he left [the central government] to serve asGovernorofYi province[益州牧].[Liu] Xiangfirst served asGrand AdministratorofJiangxia[江夏太守] during the time[Sun] Jianwas serving inChangsha[長沙], so he could not have nominated[Liu] Yanto serve asFilial and Incorrupt, that much is clear.
TheEminent Worthies of Linglingstates: When[Liu] ZhangsentFa Zhengto receiveLiu Bei,[Liu] Baprotested, “[Liu] Beiis a mighty person! If he enters, he will certainly cause trouble! Do not let him in!” But when he entered,[Liu] Baagain protested, “If you send[Liu] Beito fight againstZhang Lu, that would be like releasing a tiger into mountains and forests!”[Liu] Zhangdid not heed him.[Liu] Bathus shut his gates and claimed to be ill. When[Liu] BeiattackedChengdu[成都], he ordered his army, “If there are those who harm[Liu] Ba, they shall be executed and their clan exterminated to the third degree.” And when he obtained[Liu] Ba, he was very delighted.
TheEminent Worthies of Linglingstates:Zhang Feionce went to the residence of[Liu] Ba, but[Liu] Badid not talk with him, and so[Zhang] Feiwas resentful and furious.Zhuge Liangtold[Liu] Ba, “AlthoughZhang Feiis indeed a military man, he respects and admires you.Our Lordshipis gathering both civil and military [talents] at this time so as to complete his major cause. Though you have always been of a noble and candid nature, you should deign yourself a bit.”[Liu] Basaid, “When a great man resides in this world, he should deal with the heroic and mighty figures of theFour Seas. How could he talk together with mere soldiers?” When[Liu] Beiheard this, he angrily said, “I want to stabilize the realm under heaven, and yetZichuonly causes trouble! He wants to return north by borrowing the roads here! How could he want to achieve my undertaking?”[Liu] Beilater said, “Zichusurpasses many people in talent and wisdom. If it were me, I could appoint and utilize him, but if it were not me, they would it find it hard to even appoint him.”[Zhuge] Liangalso said, “In formulating plans within the tent,7I am far from being on par withZichu! If it comes to the beating of drums, gathering armies at the gates, and getting the common people to excel at being soldiers, then I may discuss that with other men.” WhenLiu Zhangwas previously attacked,[Liu] Beiand the soldiers made a covenant: “If this matter succeeds, I shall not involve myself in whatever things are in the warehouses.” And whenChengdu[成都] was seized, the soldiers all cast away their shields and arms, and went towards the depositories, competing to seize the treasures. There were not enough army supplies, and[Liu] Beiwas extremely worried over it.[Liu] Basaid, “This is simple! You need only to cast coins worth one hundred cash, regulate the prices, and order officials to run the government markets.”[Liu] Beiheeded him, and within several months, the warehouses were full andplenty.•••
This is presumably a metaphor for domestic affairs or clandestine strategies.↩
In the twenty-fourth year of theera [219], theFormer LordbecameKingofHanzhong[漢中王].[Liu] Bawas appointed asSecretariat Ministerand later replacedFa ZhengasDirector of the Secretariat. He personally conducted himself with purity and frugality, and he did not cultivate his own estate.8He also believed that because he was someone who submitted [recently] and was not an old [follower],9he feared he would be under suspicion, so he was respectful and quiet. When he withdrew [from court], he would not engage in any private dealings, and he would not speak if it was not a public matter.GWhen theFormer Lordclaimed the imperial title, he made evident proclamations to August Heaven, the Paramount Emperor, Mother Earth, and other deities.10All of the various texts, orders, commands, and decrees were composed by[Liu] Ba.•••
Liu Baavoided enriching himself despite his status.↩
This is sayingLiu Bahad a shorter time of service toLiu Beithan people likeZhang Fei.↩
It seems that “evident proclamations” [昭告] would have been sacrifices to the gods mentioned in the text.↩
TheEminent Worthies of Linglingstates: At the time, the feelings of the in the central plains were not unified.11When they heard[Liu] Beiwas in Shu [蜀], all directions craned their necks [eagerly]. Yet, when[Liu] Beihad firm intentions to assume the legitimate,12[Liu] Babelieved doing that would show the world that he was not lenient, and wanted him to delay. He andMaster of RecordsYong Maoprotested thus to[Liu] Bei.[Liu] Beilater killed[Yong] Maodue to some other matter. From this, those from afar stoppedcoming.•••
That is to say that many people in the regions where theWei kingdomgoverned were not content being under them.↩
Here, “assume the legitimate” [即真] is the same as declaring oneself emperor.↩
In the second year of theera [222], he died. After he died, theVice Secretariat MinisterofWei,Chen Qun, wrote a letter toAssisting ChancellorZhuge Liang, asking about news regarding[Liu] Baand calling him “LordLiuZichu”. He was extremely respected likeso.H
TheEminent Worthies of Linglingstates: TheGeneral who Assists Wu,Zhang Zhao, once replied toSun Quanby discussing the narrow-mindness of[Liu] Ba, that he should not have refusedZhang Feiin such an extreme manner.[Sun] Quansaid, “Supposing ifZichufollowed the ebbs and flows13of the world, and he charmedXuandeby associating with anunbecoming man, how would he be worthy of being called a noblegentleman?”•••
Here, “ebbs and flows” is a metaphor for trends.Liu Badid not follow popular trends and remained himself.↩
